project.name: ai-bookmarks
project.description: AI Bookmark Manager is a web application that allows managing and organizing bookmarks exported from Twitter (X), using Google Gemini AI to automatically analyze, categorize, and title AI-related tweets in Catalan.

nodes.name: deploy-to-vps.sh
nodes.summary: Bash deployment script that syncs the local dist/ build to a VPS over SSH/rsync, creating a timestamped backup of the remote directory before overwriting. Targets ailinksdb.masellas.info.

nodes.name: eslint.config.js
nodes.summary: Root ESLint flat config enabling TypeScript, React Hooks, and React Refresh rules for all .ts/.tsx files, with dist/ excluded from linting.

nodes.name: api-DyDpKlVl.js
nodes.summary: Minified build artifact bundling the shared API layer: config constants, UI string constants (Catalan locale), and async functions for fetching/saving bookmarks and categories, plus a Claude AI categorization proxy call.

nodes.name: popup-DbKNwE_q.js
nodes.summary: Minified build artifact for the extension popup UI, bundling the full React popup component tree. Imports from the api build artifact.

nodes.name: service-worker.js
nodes.summary: Minified build artifact of the Chrome extension service worker. Handles CHECK_DUPLICATE, SAVE_BOOKMARK, ADD_CATEGORY, and GET_CATEGORIES messages from the popup, with in-memory category caching (5-minute TTL).

nodes.name: service-worker.ts
nodes.summary: Chrome extension service worker source. Listens for popup messages (CHECK_DUPLICATE, SAVE_BOOKMARK, ADD_CATEGORY, GET_CATEGORIES) and dispatches to the shared API layer. Maintains an in-memory category cache with 5-minute TTL to reduce API round-trips.

nodes.name: getCachedCategories
nodes.summary: Async function that returns categories from an in-memory cache if fresh (< 5 min old), otherwise fetches from the API and updates the cache. Falls back to stale cache or ['Altres'] on error.

nodes.name: content.js
nodes.summary: Minified build artifact of the content script. Extracts page metadata (title, description, author, URL) from DOM/meta tags and responds to GET_METADATA messages from the extension popup.

nodes.name: content.ts
nodes.summary: Chrome extension content script source. Scrapes page metadata (title, description, author, URL) from OG/Twitter/standard meta tags with fallback strategies, and exposes it via a chrome.runtime message listener responding to GET_METADATA.

nodes.name: extractMetadata
nodes.summary: Extracts page title, description, author, and URL from the DOM using OG tags, Twitter meta tags, standard meta tags, and plain HTML elements as fallbacks. Returns an ExtractedMetadata object.

nodes.name: index.tsx
nodes.summary: Entry point for the Chrome extension popup. Mounts the Popup React component into the DOM root using ReactDOM.createRoot inside React.StrictMode.

nodes.name: popup.tsx
nodes.summary: Main React component for the browser extension popup. Manages view-state machine (loading, form, duplicate, success, error, tabs, tabs-saving, tabs-summary), handles single-page bookmark saving with AI category suggestion via Claude proxy, and drives bulk tab saving with sequential processing, per-tab status tracking, and retry support.

nodes.name: Popup
nodes.summary: Default-exported React component implementing the full extension popup UI. Controls view-state transitions, form state persistence via chrome.storage.session, and orchestrates all user interactions.

nodes.name: loadTabsData
nodes.summary: Loads all open tabs from the current window, resolves tab group metadata, filters chrome:// URLs, marks already-saved tabs by comparing against stored bookmarks, and fetches existing categories.

nodes.name: loadData
nodes.summary: Loads metadata for the active tab via content script injection, checks for duplicates, fetches categories, and calls the Claude proxy for AI-suggested categories and enriched title/description.

nodes.name: handleBulkSave
nodes.summary: Sequentially saves selected tabs as bookmarks: extracts tweet body via scripting for Twitter/X URLs, calls Claude proxy for categorization, constructs bookmark objects, saves via background message, and tracks per-tab status.

nodes.name: singleSaveUtils.ts
nodes.summary: Exports a single utility function implementing a 3-tier category resolution fallback: Claude-validated categories, user-selected categories, or the default 'Altres'.

nodes.name: resolveSaveCategories
nodes.summary: Resolves final bookmark categories using 3-tier fallback: AI categories filtered by whitelist, user-selected categories, or fallback 'Altres'.

nodes.name: tabsUtils.ts
nodes.summary: Collection of pure utility functions and constants supporting the tabs bulk-save feature: tab filtering by group, selection management (toggle/select-all/deselect-all), bookmark construction from a tab, save status summarization, favicon URL generation, and a Tailwind CSS color map for Chrome tab group colors.

nodes.name: filterTabsByGroup
nodes.summary: Filters tab list by group filter value: all tabs, ungrouped tabs, or a specific group ID.

nodes.name: buildTabBookmark
nodes.summary: Constructs a Bookmark object from a TabItem with a unique ID and author resolved from the URL.

nodes.name: getTabSaveSummary
nodes.summary: Counts saved and failed tabs from the status map to produce a summary object.

nodes.name: getFaviconUrl
nodes.summary: Generates a Chrome extension favicon URL for a given page URL using the internal _favicon endpoint.

nodes.name: postcss.config.js
nodes.summary: PostCSS configuration for the extension, enabling Tailwind CSS and Autoprefixer plugins.

nodes.name: api.ts
nodes.summary: Shared API client for the browser extension. Provides typed functions for fetching bookmarks, categories, saving bookmarks and categories via REST API, duplicate URL detection, and calling the local Claude proxy for AI-based categorization with graceful error fallback.

nodes.name: apiRequest
nodes.summary: Generic typed HTTP request helper that constructs URLs from API_CONFIG, attaches auth headers, serializes request body, and throws on non-OK responses.

nodes.name: saveBookmark
nodes.summary: Saves a bookmark by fetching all existing bookmarks, appending the new one, and POSTing the complete array. Note: this GET-modify-POST pattern is not atomic — callers must avoid concurrent calls.

nodes.name: callClaudeProxy
nodes.summary: Calls the local Claude proxy to get AI-suggested categories, title, and description for a URL. Always resolves (never throws), returning empty categories on proxy unreachability or timeout.

nodes.name: getBookmarks
nodes.summary: Fetches all bookmarks from the REST API.

nodes.name: getCategories
nodes.summary: Fetches the category list from the REST API.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Central configuration module for the extension exporting API base URL and auth secret, Catalan error message strings, the Claude proxy URL, and all Catalan UI strings including dynamic tab-count message generators.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Shared TypeScript type definitions for the extension: Bookmark, ExtractedMetadata, Chrome messaging types (Message, MessageResponse), API response shapes, and tabs-feature types (TabItem, TabSaveStatus, TabGroupColor, TabGroupInfo).

nodes.name: tailwind.config.js
nodes.summary: Tailwind CSS configuration for the extension, scoped to popup directory HTML/JS/TS/TSX files.

nodes.name: single-save.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for resolveSaveCategories covering all 3-tier fallback branches: Claude categories in whitelist, Claude categories filtered out, empty Claude result, empty user selection, and partial whitelist filtering.

nodes.name: tabs-filter.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for tab filtering utilities. Tests filterTabsByGroup (all tabs, ungrouped-only, specific groupId) and hasGroups from tabsUtils. Uses a local makeTabs factory for isolated test data.

nodes.name: tabs-save.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for tab save helper functions. Covers buildTabBookmark (id prefix, url mapping, default categories, timestamp) and getTabSaveSummary (counting saved/failed statuses from a Map).

nodes.name: tabs-selection.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for tab selection utilities. Tests toggleTabSelection (add/remove/immutability), selectAllVisible, deselectAllVisible, and getSelectionCount from tabsUtils.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration for the browser extension. Defines three entry points (popup HTML, content script TS, service worker TS) with custom output path rules and a manualChunks strategy to inline shared modules into the service worker and content script.

nodes.name: vitest.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest configuration for the extension test suite. Sets jsdom as the test environment and enables global test APIs.

nodes.name: deploy.sh
nodes.summary: Bash deployment script for the mobile PWA. Builds the project via npm then rsync-transfers the dist/ output to the VPS path /var/www/ailinksdb.masellas.info/mobile/ with --delete to clean stale files.

nodes.name: postcss.config.js
nodes.summary: PostCSS configuration for the mobile PWA. Enables Tailwind CSS and autoprefixer plugins.

nodes.name: sw.js
nodes.summary: Minimal PWA service worker. Handles install (skipWaiting), activate (clients.claim), and fetch (passthrough) events to satisfy PWA installability requirements without any caching strategy.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Root React component for the mobile PWA share-target flow. Manages a ViewState machine (loading/form/duplicate/success/error), loads shared URL/title from query params, fetches categories from the API, handles category selection and creation, validates and saves bookmarks with duplicate detection.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Default-exported React component. Orchestrates the full bookmark save UI with a state machine across loading, form, duplicate, success and error views. Handles category fetch, selection toggling, adding new categories, duplicate detection, and bookmark persistence.

nodes.name: loadData
nodes.summary: Async function called on mount. Parses share URL/title from query params, fetches categories from the API (falls back to ['Altres']), then transitions view to 'form'.

nodes.name: handleAddCategory
nodes.summary: Validates and adds a new category locally, then auto-selects it. Does not persist to the server — purely client-side state update.

nodes.name: handleSave
nodes.summary: Validates title length and URL presence, checks for duplicates via API, constructs a Bookmark object with a mob_ prefixed id, and calls saveBookmark. Transitions view state accordingly.

nodes.name: api.ts
nodes.summary: Thin re-export barrel for the shared extension API. Forwards getBookmarks, getCategories, saveBookmark, and isDuplicate from the shared extension module so the mobile PWA can consume the same API surface without duplication.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Mobile PWA configuration constants: ERRORS object with Catalan-language user-facing error strings, and UI_STRINGS object with all static UI label and message strings. Serves as the single source of truth for i18n text in the mobile app.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: PWA entry point: registers the service worker at /mobile/sw.js if supported, then mounts the React App component into the DOM root using React 18 createRoot with StrictMode.

nodes.name: utils.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for mobile utility functions. Covers resolveAuthorFromUrl (GitHub, Twitter/X, and fallback cases) and parseShareParams (url param, text param fallback, and empty input cases).

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Mobile PWA utility functions: resolveAuthorFromUrl infers a source label (github/twitter/web) from a URL string; parseShareParams extracts url and title from a Web Share Target query string, falling back to the text param for the URL.

nodes.name: resolveAuthorFromUrl
nodes.summary: Infers a source/author label string ('github', 'twitter', or 'web') from a given URL by testing regex patterns.

nodes.name: parseShareParams
nodes.summary: Parses Web Share Target query string parameters into a ShareParams object with url and title fields, using the text param as a fallback for url.

nodes.name: tailwind.config.js
nodes.summary: Tailwind CSS configuration for the mobile PWA workspace. Scans HTML and all TypeScript/TSX/JS sources under src/ for class names; no theme extensions or plugins configured.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration for the mobile PWA: uses the React plugin, sets the public base path to /mobile/, outputs to dist/, and configures the jsdom test environment for Vitest.

nodes.name: postcss.config.js
nodes.summary: Root PostCSS configuration enabling Tailwind CSS and Autoprefixer plugins for the main web app workspace.

nodes.name: install.sh
nodes.summary: macOS LaunchAgent installer script for the local Claude AI proxy server. Detects the Node.js binary path, substitutes path placeholders in a .plist template, writes the plist to ~/Library/LaunchAgents, and loads it via launchctl so the proxy starts on port 3838 at login.

nodes.name: server.js
nodes.summary: Express.js proxy server that bridges the React app to the Claude AI CLI. Exposes two POST endpoints: /categorize (handles both tweet URLs and regular pages, generating title/description/categories via Claude) and /process-tweet (processes raw tweet objects into structured bookmark data). Uses child_process.spawn to invoke the claude CLI with JSON schema-constrained output. Designed for testability: createApp factory separates construction from port binding, and getChildEnv strips Claude Code session variables to allow nested invocation.

nodes.name: getChildEnv
nodes.summary: Strips CLAUDECODE and CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT env vars from the process environment so the claude CLI can be spawned from within a Claude Code session. Accepts an optional envObject for testability.

nodes.name: createApp
nodes.summary: Factory function that creates and configures the Express application. Contains the internal callClaude helper (spawns claude CLI subprocess with timeout handling) and registers /categorize and /process-tweet routes. Accepts claudeBin and claudeTimeout for testability.

nodes.name: sanitizeText
nodes.summary: Sanitizes tweet text by stripping hashtags, mentions, control characters, and normalizing whitespace. Truncates to 700 characters. Ported from geminiService.ts.

nodes.name: mock-claude.sh
nodes.summary: Minimal bash mock of the claude CLI binary used in proxy tests. Inspects argument string to decide which JSON response shape to echo: categorization response or tweet processing response. Avoids spawning the real Claude CLI during automated tests.

nodes.name: test-install.sh
nodes.summary: Bash test script that validates the launchd plist template (com.ailinks.claude-proxy.plist) by substituting __HOME__ and __PROXY_DIR__ placeholders and verifying no placeholders remain and the actual HOME path is present. Exits with SKIP if the plist template does not yet exist.

nodes.name: split-tweets.sh
nodes.summary: Bash utility that splits a large Twitter bookmark JSON file into batches of 10 tweets, sorted chronologically (oldest first). Uses an embedded Python 3 script for JSON parsing and date handling. Supports both Unix timestamp and Twitter date string formats. Creates a named output directory with zero-padded numbered JSON files.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: The central React component of the AI Bookmark Manager (1909 lines). Manages the full application lifecycle: importing Twitter JSON archives, processing tweets through the Claude proxy, deduplication/blacklist logic, category management with drag-and-drop reordering, bookmark CRUD (create/edit/delete), highlight toggling, search with URL sync, data persistence via storage service, JSON export/backup, and a multi-step review carousel for non-AI tweets. Also defines the BookmarkCard sub-component for rendering individual bookmarks with neobrutalist styling.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Default export. Root React component orchestrating all application state (20+ useState/useRef hooks), effects for data loading and persistence, and rendering the full UI including header, sidebar navigation, category sections, modals, search, and review carousel.

nodes.name: BookmarkCard
nodes.summary: React functional component rendering a single bookmark card with neobrutalist styling. Displays categories as badges, author/date metadata, title, description, external links, and action buttons for edit, delete, and highlight toggle.

nodes.name: processTweetsData
nodes.summary: Core async function that deduplicates tweets against existing bookmarks and blacklist, calls processBookmarksWithClaude for AI processing, maps AI results to Bookmark objects (extracting author, date, description, external links), and triggers the review modal for non-AI tweets.

nodes.name: handleFileUpload
nodes.summary: Handles JSON file input: detects whether the file is a backup (merges categories/bookmarks/deletedIds) or a Twitter archive (extracts tweet array and calls processTweetsData). Uses FileReader API with AbortController for cancellation.

nodes.name: groupedBookmarks
nodes.summary: useMemo computation that groups bookmarks by all their categories (a bookmark can appear in multiple groups), sorts each group newest-first, and ensures uncategorized bookmarks fall into the default group.

nodes.name: ScrollToTop.tsx
nodes.summary: React component that renders a fixed-position scroll-to-top button (neobrutalist yellow with ArrowUp icon). Becomes visible when the user scrolls more than 400px down, using a scroll event listener attached/detached via useEffect.

nodes.name: ScrollToTop
nodes.summary: Exported React FC that tracks scroll position and conditionally renders a smooth-scroll-to-top button.

nodes.name: UI.tsx
nodes.summary: Design system primitives for the neobrutalist UI. Exports: Button (4 variants: primary/secondary/danger/ghost with shadow/active effects), Card (bordered container with optional title), Input, TextArea, Label, Select (with custom SVG chevron), Badge (colored pill), and Modal (backdrop + z-index priority support). All components use Tailwind CSS utility classes.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: React application entry point. Mounts the App component inside React.StrictMode onto the DOM element with id='root'. Throws an error if the root element is not found.

nodes.name: claudeService.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest test suite for claudeService.ts covering: module export shape verification, successful proxy response mapping to ProcessedTweetResult[], fallback bookmark creation when proxy is unreachable (fetch failure), and AbortError propagation when AbortSignal is pre-aborted. Uses vi.stubGlobal to mock fetch and importMeta.env.

nodes.name: claudeService.ts
nodes.summary: Handles communication with the local Claude proxy server to process raw tweets. Iterates tweets one at a time (BATCH_SIZE=1), sanitizes text, POSTs to /process-tweet with retry logic (up to 3 attempts), and generates fallback results when the proxy is unavailable.

nodes.name: sanitizeText
nodes.summary: Strips hashtags, mentions, control characters, and truncates tweet text to 700 characters before sending to the proxy.

nodes.name: processBookmarksWithClaude
nodes.summary: Exported async function that orchestrates batch processing of raw tweets through the Claude proxy. Supports abort signals, progress callbacks, retry logic with delays, and produces fallback entries on failure.

nodes.name: storage.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a unified storage abstraction that routes reads/writes to either a VPS REST API (when VITE_STORAGE_SECRET is set) or browser localStorage. Manages bookmarks, categories, and deleted IDs with get/save/clear operations.

nodes.name: apiRequest
nodes.summary: Generic authenticated fetch helper for the VPS storage API. Builds URL from env config, attaches x-api-secret header, and throws on non-OK responses.

nodes.name: translations.ts
nodes.summary: Centralised i18n strings for the UI, primarily in Catalan. Covers app labels, log messages, modal text, alert confirmations, default category lists, and the AI system prompt template used for Gemini/Claude categorization.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the core TypeScript interfaces for the application: TweetRaw (raw Twitter export shape), Bookmark (persisted entity), Category (string alias), ProcessedTweetResult (AI output), and LogEntry (import log).

nodes.name: vite-env.d.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript ambient declarations for Vite's import.meta.env, exposing VITE_API_KEY, VITE_STORAGE_API_URL, and VITE_STORAGE_SECRET as typed environment variables.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite configuration for the main web app. Enables React plugin, sets dev server to port 3000 on all interfaces, injects GEMINI_API_KEY into process.env, and adds a root path alias '@'.

nodes.name: tailwind.config.js
nodes.summary: Root Tailwind CSS configuration. Scans all HTML and src files for class usage with no custom theme extensions or plugins.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Root Vite/Vitest configuration file. Enables the React plugin and sets the test environment to 'node'.

layers.name: Web App Layer
layers.description: Main React web application for bookmark management. Includes the full-featured UI (import, AI categorization, filtering, search, sync), UI primitive components, service clients for AI and storage, i18n translations, and core type definitions.

layers.name: Chrome Extension Layer
layers.description: Chrome browser extension with popup UI, background service worker, content script, shared API client, shared type definitions, and build artifacts. Enables saving bookmarks directly from the browser. Includes source files, compiled artifacts, and extension-specific configuration.

layers.name: Mobile PWA Layer
layers.description: Mobile Progressive Web App enabling bookmark saving via the native Web Share Target API. Includes the main React component, PWA service worker for offline support, URL/share-parameter utilities, configuration, and deployment script.

layers.name: Proxy Backend Layer
layers.description: Local Express.js proxy server that bridges the browser app to the Claude CLI. Exposes REST endpoints for AI categorization requests and streams responses. Includes installation scripts and test utilities for the proxy.

layers.name: Infrastructure & Config Layer
layers.description: Root-level configuration, tooling, and utility scripts for the monorepo. Includes deployment scripts, ESLint config, PostCSS/Tailwind/Vite configs for the main app, and the Twitter bookmark batch-splitting utility.

tour.title: Web App Entry Point
tour.description: Start here: src/main.tsx is the minimal React bootstrapping file that mounts the entire web application into the DOM. It has zero incoming dependencies (fan-in: 0), making it the true root of the web app's dependency graph. Understanding this file sets the stage for everything else — it delegates all real work to App.tsx immediately.
tour.languageLesson: React entry points typically call ReactDOM.createRoot().render() with a single root component. The simplicity is intentional — this file's only job is to start the engine.

tour.title: The Core Domain Model
tour.description: Before diving into the main component, understand the data shapes that flow through the entire system. src/types.ts defines the three foundational types — Bookmark, Category, and BookmarkGroup — and is imported by App.tsx, the storage service, and the Claude service (fan-in: 3). Knowing these types makes every other file immediately legible.
tour.languageLesson: In TypeScript, co-locating core domain types in a dedicated file prevents circular imports and creates a stable contract that all other modules can depend on without depending on each other.

tour.title: The Main Web App Component
tour.description: App.tsx is the highest fan-out node in the entire codebase (fan-out: 6), orchestrating all features of the bookmark manager: importing tweets, AI categorization via Google Gemini, filtering, duplicate detection, VPS sync, and export. At 1909 lines it is the largest file; it is the central hub that connects every service, UI component, and utility in the web layer. All roads from main.tsx lead here.
tour.languageLesson: A high fan-out component like this is an orchestrator pattern — it wires subsystems together rather than implementing logic itself. The tradeoff is that it becomes a complexity hotspot requiring careful state management.

tour.title: UI Primitive Design System
tour.description: App.tsx delegates all visual rendering to two UI files. src/components/UI.tsx exports 7 primitive components — Badge, Button, Card, Input, Modal, Select, Tooltip — forming the design system foundation. src/components/ScrollToTop.tsx is a small utility component. Both are pure presentational leaves with no outgoing dependencies, making them safe to study and modify in isolation.

tour.title: i18n Translations Layer
tour.description: src/translations.ts holds all user-facing string constants for Catalan and Spanish, and is consumed by both App.tsx and the storage service (fan-in: 2). This separation means the UI is never hardcoded in English — a deliberate design choice for a Catalan-language-first application. It is a leaf node with no outgoing imports.
tour.languageLesson: Keeping translation strings in a dedicated constants file (rather than inline JSX) makes it trivial to add new locales and keeps components free of language-specific logic.

tour.title: Persistence: Storage Service
tour.description: src/services/storage.ts manages where bookmarks and categories actually live: a VPS API with local browser storage as fallback. It imports both src/types.ts (for data shapes) and src/translations.ts (for error messages surfaced to users). Understanding this service explains how the app survives network failures gracefully.

tour.title: AI Integration: Claude Service
tour.description: src/services/claudeService.ts is the web app's bridge to local AI. It communicates with the Claude CLI proxy (covered later) to perform bookmark categorization. It consumes src/types.ts for the Bookmark shape and has its own test file (claudeService.test.ts), making it the most rigorously tested module in the web layer. This is the core AI capability of the whole system.

tour.title: The AI Proxy Backend
tour.description: proxy/server.js is a standalone Express.js server that acts as the bridge between browser-based JavaScript (which cannot spawn CLI processes) and the local Claude CLI binary. It exposes /api/claude and /api/claude/stream endpoints. The claudeService.ts from the previous step calls this proxy. This file is architecturally isolated — zero fan-in and zero fan-out in the graph — meaning it is a self-contained backend.
tour.languageLesson: Browser security restrictions (CORS, process spawning) necessitate a local proxy pattern. The proxy is intentionally minimal: it does one thing (spawn claude CLI and stream output) and does it well.

tour.title: Chrome Extension: Shared Foundation
tour.description: The Chrome extension is a separate sub-application that shares data contracts with the mobile PWA. Its foundation is two files: extension/shared/types.ts (fan-in: 5 — the most depended-upon file in the entire codebase) defines Bookmark, Category, and SaveBookmarkRequest; extension/shared/config.ts holds API endpoint constants. These shared files are depended on by the popup, service worker, content script, and even the mobile app.

tour.title: Chrome Extension: Shared API Client
tour.description: extension/shared/api.ts (fan-in: 3) is the HTTP client shared across the extension popup, service worker, and mobile PWA. It provides async functions for fetching/saving bookmarks and categories, plus the Claude AI proxy call. Its position as a hub between three different consumers — and as the module that mobile/src/api.ts re-exports verbatim — makes it the glue between all client surfaces.

tour.title: Chrome Extension: Service Worker & Content Script
tour.description: The extension's background process is extension/background/service-worker.ts, which handles Chrome message-passing (CHECK_DUPLICATE, SAVE_BOOKMARK, ADD_CATEGORY, GET_CATEGORIES) and maintains a category cache. Separately, extension/content/content.ts is injected directly into Twitter/X pages to extract bookmark data from the DOM. Together these two files form the invisible backend of the extension, running outside any visible UI.
tour.languageLesson: Chrome extensions have three isolated JavaScript contexts: content scripts (page DOM access), service workers (background persistence), and the popup (UI). They communicate exclusively via Chrome's message-passing API — they cannot share memory.

tour.title: Chrome Extension: Popup UI
tour.description: extension/popup/index.tsx is the extension popup's entry point, mirroring the role of src/main.tsx for the web app. It mounts extension/popup/popup.tsx, the 823-line popup component that manages the full bookmark-saving flow: single-page save, multi-tab batch save, category selection, and duplicate detection. The popup delegates save logic to singleSaveUtils.ts and tabsUtils.ts to keep its component code focused on UI state.

tour.title: Mobile PWA Entry & App
tour.description: The mobile Progressive Web App is the third client surface. mobile/src/main.tsx bootstraps the React root (same pattern as the web app), mounting mobile/src/App.tsx which implements the Web Share Target flow — when a user shares a Twitter/X URL from their phone's browser, the OS routes it here. The mobile app then displays bookmarks and submits to the same VPS API used by the web app.

tour.title: Mobile PWA: Code Reuse via Barrel Re-export
tour.description: mobile/src/api.ts is a single-line barrel file that re-exports everything from extension/shared/api.ts. This is the architecture's most important code-reuse decision: rather than duplicating the HTTP client, the mobile app transparently delegates to the extension's shared API layer. mobile/src/config.ts and mobile/src/utils.ts provide mobile-specific configuration and utility functions to complete the mobile layer.
tour.languageLesson: A barrel re-export (`export * from '../other/module'`) lets two sub-applications share implementation without coupling their internal paths. The mobile app can evolve its import paths independently of the extension's internals.
